message:
fix(shadow): include start_id in process identity hash to prevent PID-reuse collisions

The compute_identity_hash function in the shadow mode observer was
hashing only (uid, comm, cmd) to create a stable identity for tracking
processes across PID reuse. This was insufficient — two different
processes with the same user, command name, and arguments but different
start times would produce identical identity hashes, causing the shadow
observer to conflate them.

Fix: add proc.start_id (boot-qualified start time) to the hash input,
making the identity hash unique across process lifetimes even when PID
is recycled. This aligns with the TOCTOU defense strategy described in
the plan (process_triage-o8m) where identity tuples must include
start_id.

Changes:
- crates/pt-core/src/shadow.rs: add start_id to Sha256 hash input in
  compute_identity_hash(); add test verifying different start_ids
  produce different hashes
- crates/pt-telemetry/src/shadow.rs: update doc comment on
  Observation.identity_hash to reflect the new hash composition
  (start_id, user, command)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
